abbreviations.definition: invisible decrease (like sc2tog, but only in the front loops) Invisible Decrease Tutorial

materials: 1 (1, 1, 2, 2) skeins of yarn from the Knitted Knocker approved yarn list.
Size G (4.5mm) crochet hook (or size needed to get gauge)
3 locking stitch markers (1 in a different color for start of round)

rounds.rawInstruction: Create a magic ring with 6 sc in the ring. Close the ring.
rounds.summary: Create a magic ring and work 6 single crochets into it.
rounds.title: Rnd 1

rounds.rawInstruction: inc x 6 (12 sc)
rounds.summary: Increase in each stitch around.
rounds.title: Rnd 2

rounds.rawInstruction: (2 sc, 2 inc, PM X 3 (18 sc)
rounds.summary: Work 2 sc, 2 inc, place marker, repeat three times.
rounds.title: Rnd 3

rounds.rawInstruction: (4 sc, 2 inc) x 3 (24 sc).Move Markers up as you go.
rounds.summary: Work 4 sc, 2 inc, repeat three times.
rounds.title: Rnd 4

rounds.rawInstruction: (7 sc, inc) x 3 (27 sc)
rounds.summary: Work 7 sc, inc, repeat three times.
rounds.title: Rnd 5

rounds.rawInstruction: (Work to 1 sc before marker and inc x 2) x 3 (33 sc)
rounds.summary: Work to 1 sc before marker, inc twice, repeat three times.
rounds.title: Rnd 6

rounds.rawInstruction: (Work to marker and inc in marked stitch) x 3 (36 sc)
rounds.summary: Work to marker, inc in marked stitch, repeat three times.
rounds.title: Rnd 7

rounds.rawInstruction: Using the tapestry needle, sew in the magic ring’s 6” tail.
rounds.summary: Weave in the magic ring tail.
rounds.title: Using the tapestry needle, sew in the magic ring’s 6” tail.

rounds.rawInstruction: Select the cup size and continue.
rounds.summary: Choose cup size and follow corresponding instructions.
rounds.title: Select the cup size and continue.

rounds.rawInstruction: A cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 three times until 4.5” across or 13 rounds (approx. 63 sc)
rounds.summary: For A cup, repeat rounds 6 and 7 until reaching 13 total rounds.
rounds.title: A cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7

rounds.rawInstruction: B cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 four times until 5” across or 15 rounds (approx.72 sc)
rounds.summary: For B cup, repeat rounds 6 and 7 until reaching 15 total rounds.
rounds.title: B cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7

rounds.rawInstruction: C cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 five times until 5.5” across or 17 rounds (approx. 81 sc)
rounds.summary: For C cup, repeat rounds 6 and 7 until reaching 17 total rounds.
rounds.title: C cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7

rounds.rawInstruction: D cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 six times until 6” across or 19 rounds (approx 90 sc)
rounds.summary: For D cup, repeat rounds 6 and 7 until reaching 19 total rounds.
rounds.title: D cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7

rounds.rawInstruction: DD cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 seven times until 6.5” across or 21 rounds (approx. 99 sc)
rounds.summary: For DD cup, repeat rounds 6 and 7 until reaching 21 total rounds.
rounds.title: DD cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7

rounds.rawInstruction: Work 2 rnds even through the back loop only.
rounds.summary: Work two rounds of single crochet in the back loop only, without increasing.
rounds.title: Work 2 rnds even through the back loop only.

rounds.rawInstruction: When the size is correct, the turning rows have been completed, and you’re ready to start decreasing, crochet until 1 sc before the first marker.
rounds.summary: Work even until 1 stitch before the first marker.
rounds.title: When the size is correct, the turning rows have been completed, and you’re ready to start decreasing, crochet until 1 sc before the first marker.

rounds.rawInstruction: Rnd 1: (inv dec (PM), inv dec, then sc until 1 sc before the next marker) x 3.
rounds.summary: Decrease round: inv dec, place marker, inv dec, sc to 1 before next marker, repeat three times.
rounds.title: Rnd 1

rounds.rawInstruction: Repeat Rnd 1 until you have 15 sc left.
rounds.summary: Repeat the decrease round until only 15 stitches remain.
rounds.title: Repeat Rnd 1

rounds.rawInstruction: Cut yarn leaving a 10” tail.
rounds.summary: Cut the yarn, leaving a long tail.
rounds.title: Cut yarn leaving a 10” tail.

rounds.rawInstruction: Important! Do Not Miss This Step! Thread tapestry needle and weave through the front loop only for the remaining 15 sc.
rounds.summary: Thread the tail and weave through the front loops of the remaining stitches.
rounds.title: Important! Do Not Miss This Step! Thread tapestry needle and weave through the front loop only for the remaining 15 sc.

rounds.rawInstruction: If stuffing the Knocker: Slightly overstuff with Poly-fil (or other fiber fill) Do not close hole as it is difficult to reopen. Stuffing will not come out when worn or washed. Tuck tail into Knocker so that filling can be adjusted as needed.
rounds.summary: Overstuff the knocker and tuck the tail inside.
rounds.title: If stuffing the Knocker

rounds.rawInstruction: If mailing in unstuffed: Leave the hole open and the yarn tail outside of the Knocker. If your yarn tends to fray easily, tie a loose knot at the end of the tail.
rounds.summary: For mailing unstuffed, leave the hole open and tail outside.
rounds.title: If mailing in unstuffed
